name: Greenwich Village Brownstone Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Pecan halves or large pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter an 8-inch square cake pan and line it with wax paper. Butter the paper and flour the pan.
3. Melt the chocolate in the top of a double boiler over hot water until smooth. Remove from heat.
4. Beat the butter in a large bowl until light. Add the vanilla, both sugars, and corn syrup. Mix well.
5. Add the eggs one at a time, beating thoroughly after each addition. Stir in the melted chocolate, flour, salt, and 1 cup of pecans.
6. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and sprinkle the remaining pecans on top.
7. Bake until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean, about 1 hour. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 30 minutes.
8. Cover the pan with another rack and invert it. Remove from the paper and invert back onto a wire rack. Let cool completely before cutting into bars. Makes about 24 brownies.
